Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 09:15:12 -0700
Subject: Spike in latency + 5xxs impacting checkout (US-west)

Jordan —

We saw a large spike in end-to-end latency across our checkout service starting ~08:40 PDT and a surge of 500 responses from Redwood between 08:47–09:05. This impacted about 12% of traffic and caused retries on our side. We’ve attached a short timeline and sample logs.

Can your team confirm if this was an infra/regression issue on the Redwood side and what mitigations you have in place? This is blocking our morning releases — we need an initial readout and ETA for mitigation.

Elena Ruiz
Head of Platform Engineering
MapleStreet

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Cc: Samantha Price <samantha.price@cloudforge.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Cross-cloud evaluation playbook + next steps (post-demo)

Team —

Quick recap from this morning's call with CloudForge (Samantha) and a tentative action plan. High-level takeaways:

- CloudForge is running initial comparisons against two hyperscaler inference endpoints (HyperscaleA, HyperEdge) and saw p95 deltas that worry their infra team for real-time scoring (p95 ~210–290ms on HyperEdge vs ~110–140ms on Redwood-hosted variant in our demo). They want a reproducible A/B run.
- Primary ask: a concise A/B protocol they can run (or we run jointly) to measure p95/p99 and sustained tokens/sec at 256/1024/2048 sequence lengths.
- Secondary ask: cost-per-million-tokens estimate...

text:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Moreno <alex.moreno@novaly.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Discovery — embeddings + rerank + streaming funcalls (post-call recap)

Alex — thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap while it's fresh and a proposed set of decisions so we can lock an implementation path. TL;DR: you want a deterministic rerank/embedding pipeline for patient record retrieval, predictable latency for 95th pct, and a lightweight function-calling surface for structured output to downstream EHR processors.

Key pain points you mentioned:
- Search recall variability across long clinical notes (embeddings drift across updates).
- Token-level cost spikes during peak triage windows (evening PST).
- Need for a single, auditable structured output schema for downstream ingestion into your CDW.

Claire — thanks again for taking the time this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we don’t lose momentum:

- What we heard: Fintrax needs a productionized summarization + entity-extraction pipeline for transaction narratives (high precision on AML-risk flags, low hallucination tolerance). Priority: accuracy at scale, deterministic behavior under updates.

- Recommended models to evaluate (based on today's prompts & latency targets):

Alice — thanks again for taking the time yesterday. As discussed, ACME is preparing to onboard its first private deployment and we want to align on a recommended connectivity pattern that balances latency, operational ownership, and auditability.

I attached a short comparison (redwood-connection-comparison-v2.pdf) but high level the three paths we typically see are: 
- VPC peering (cross-account peering) — low-latency, simple routing but requires both sides to accept peering and route coordination.
- AWS PrivateLink (interface endpoints) — strong isolation and per-service endpoints, easier cross-account access controls, but intro...
